article_title: B.C. Reg. 238/2025 – College of Pharmacists of British Columbia

briefs.title: Nicotine oral pouches under 4 mg removed from prescription-only drug schedule in BC
briefs.summary: BC has removed low-dose nicotine products (4 mg or less) delivered into the mouth via non-powered devices — like nicotine pouches — from the restricted drug schedule, meaning they no longer require a pharmacist's involvement to sell.
briefs.action_required: Check if you sell low-dose oral nicotine products and confirm updated sales rules with your supplier or a compliance advisor.
briefs.article_title: B.C. Reg. 238/2025 – College of Pharmacists of British Columbia
